search_queries_used: site:who.int WHO RHR 14.26 documenting partner violence LIVES history examination
site:rcem.ac.uk domestic abuse emergency department March 2015 disclosure records
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic police, shelter, safeguarding or referral action
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: RCEM states evidence is insufficient for universal screening; enquiry must be clinician-led and confidential when suspicion exists.
Action depends on consent, immediate safety, children, applicable law and local pathways.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: WHO evidence primarily addresses women experiencing intimate-partner or sexual violence; men, people under 18, elders, dependent adults and non-partner violence require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Health-care and emergency encounters where disclosure or clinician suspicion exists.
UAE_applicability: Core survivor-centred documentation principles transfer, but UAE consent, confidentiality, mandatory reporting, police, safeguarding, social support and language pathways govern.
recency_verification: WHO 2014 and RCEM 2015 official documents were reviewed on 2026-07-14; both have material recency and jurisdiction limitations.
superseded_check: No direct replacement for WHO/RHR/14.26 or newer exact RCEM domestic-abuse guideline was identified; newer WHO training material was related but not substituted.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO first-line-support section supports documenting the person’s concerns or goals, associated needs, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed safety context only when discussed; it does not assert violence or mandate action.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO documentation section supports a consent-based record of complaints, symptoms, signs or injuries, completed risk discussion and clinician-entered plan only when actually discussed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact history and examination section supports recording focused examination and general appearance only when consented to and performed; it does not mandate a sexual-assault examination.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCEM disclosure section supports recording safeguarding concern, specialist discussion and handover or follow-up only when disclosure occurred and actions were completed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Recognition and clinically indicated direct enquiry were reviewed; the source does not support universal screening.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

unresolved_source_gaps: Population beyond adult women, safe interpreter use, perpetrator relationship, children/dependants, immediate danger, coercive control, sexual violence, strangulation, pregnancy, injury details, forensic examination, reporting law and local referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
